items.headline: City seeking community input on federal housing grants
items.summary: The Housing and Human Services Commission is presenting applications for Community Development Block Grant (CDBG) funding, which supports affordable housing and community services. This is a chance to see what local housing projects are being proposed and potentially compete for federal dollars.

items.headline: Mixed-use housing project approved at 490 East Middlefield Road
items.summary: The city council approved a development agreement for a mixed-use residential development on East Middlefield Road. This project will add housing to Mountain View and was in its second reading, indicating it's moving toward final approval.

items.headline: Mountain View applies for state housing incentive funding
items.summary: The city is applying for Round 4 of California's Prohousing Incentive Program, which provides state funding to cities that remove housing barriers. This could help Mountain View accelerate housing development and reduce zoning restrictions.

items.headline: City seeks public input on $HUD housing funds for next year
items.summary: Santa Clara is opening a public comment period on how it will spend federal HUD grant money for housing and community development in fiscal year 2026/27. This is your chance to weigh in on local housing priorities.

items.headline: Safe parking program community meeting scheduled
items.summary: Sunnyvale is holding a public meeting to discuss a safe parking program, likely an initiative to provide designated overnight parking for people experiencing homelessness. This is an emerging policy that affects how the city addresses vehicle homelessness.

items.headline: Station Area Task Force reviewing land use plan and design
items.summary: A task force is reviewing the land use plan and design guidelines for the Station Area (likely near the Caltrain/VTA transit hub). This could affect what gets built and how the area develops around transit.

items.headline: City hosting public meeting on zero-waste initiatives
items.summary: Sunnyvale's Sustainability Commission is hosting a community meeting focused on 'Smart Steps Towards Zero Waste,' indicating the city is advancing its environmental goals and may be introducing new waste reduction programs or policies.

items.headline: Major housing development planned at 10333 N. Wolfe Road
items.summary: The City Council held a study session on a new housing project at this location. This is a concrete development proposal worth following if you care about housing supply and how Cupertino's neighborhoods are changing.

items.headline: City approves $5.8 million radio upgrade for all departments
items.summary: Palo Alto is spending millions on new communication equipment for police, fire, and other city services over the next 8+ years. This is a significant infrastructure investment affecting emergency response capabilities.

items.headline: Historic building tax breaks expanded under new zoning rules
items.summary: The city is considering allowing older buildings to get bigger or transfer development rights more easily as a preservation incentive. This could change what gets built in Palo Alto's commercial zones and how historic properties are valued.

items.headline: City facing potential lawsuit over North California Avenue property
items.summary: Palo Alto is dealing with litigation involving 156 North California Avenue, a significant enough issue to require city attorney involvement. The outcome could affect development or city liability in that area.

items.headline: El Camino Real retail preservation map gets updated
items.summary: The city amended its retail protection rules for the El Camino corridor, potentially affecting which businesses get protected or which properties can change use. This impacts the character and commercial mix of a major corridor.
